887	\begin{lemma}[L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable]
888	  \label{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}
889	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}
890	  \uses{def:gf_nagata_T, lem:gf_T_leadingcoeff_eq, lem:gf_finSuccEquiv_map_comm}
891	  % NOTE (variable convention): the project's univariate elimination uses
892	  %   MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv, which singles out variable X_0 as the polynomial
893	  %   variable over the coefficient ring MvPolynomial (Fin n) A. The choice of which of
894	  %   the d = n+1 variables is "distinguished/eliminated" is immaterial (a renaming);
895	  %   the informal prose elsewhere says "last variable X_d" but the Lean uses X_0.
896	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
897	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1766-L1768);
898	  % the Nagata substitution is the standard device realising the support-dimension drop.
899	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
900	  % "Therefore, the dimension of the support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$
901	  %  as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less than $n$. Hence by
902	  %  induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),"
903	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
904	  %   A_g := Localization.Away g; map := MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A A_g).
905	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
906	  %       (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
907	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
908	  %         (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A),
909	  %         IsUnit
910	  %           (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) n
911	  %             (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff
912	  %   The automorphism e is the triangular Nagata substitution; g is the (non-zero)
913	  %   leading coefficient that becomes a unit after inversion.
914	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (support-dimension drop).}
915	  Let \(A\) be a domain, \(n \ge 0\), and \(0 \ne F \in A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\). Then there
916	  exist a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) and a
917	  non-zero \(g \in A\) such that, after inverting \(g\), the image of \(e(F)\) in
918	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\), viewed as a univariate polynomial in \(X_0\) over
919	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\), has a \emph{unit} leading coefficient. Equivalently, \(e(F)\)
920	  is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\) over \(A_g\).
921	\end{lemma}
922	
923	\begin{proof}
924	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
925	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop, Nagata normalisation).}
926	  Apply the Nagata substitution \(X_i \mapsto X_i + X_0^{r^{\,i}}\) for \(i = 1, \dots, n\)
927	  and \(X_0 \mapsto X_0\), with \(r\) larger than every exponent occurring in \(F\): it is a
928	  triangular (hence invertible) \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\).
929	  Under this substitution the distinct monomials of \(F\) acquire distinct degrees in
930	  \(X_0\), so \(e(F)\), expanded as a polynomial in \(X_0\), has a unique top-degree term
931	  whose coefficient is the coefficient \(c \in A\) of the top total-degree monomial of
932	  \(F\); since \(F \ne 0\) this \(c \ne 0\). Put \(g := c\). The degree of \(e(F)\) in
933	  \(X_0\) equals the total degree of \(F\), and its leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is the
934	  constant \(g\). Passing to \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\), the image of \(g\) is a unit, so the
935	  image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\).
936	\end{proof}

939	  \label{lem:polynomial_monic_quotient_finite}
940	  \lean{Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient}
941	  \mathlibok
942	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
943	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(f \in S[X]\) a monic polynomial. Then the quotient
944	  \(S[X]/(f)\) is a module-finite \(S\)-module (indeed free of rank \(\deg f\)).
945	\end{lemma}

969	  %  as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less than $n$. Hence by

971	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (matches the landed decl verbatim).
972	  %   Finiteness is the `RingHom.Finite` of the composite ring map
973	  %   R[X_1,…,X_n] ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n] ↠ R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)
974	  %   (constant inclusion X_j ↦ X_{j+1} = MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ, then

976	  %     (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
977	  %       (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
978	  %       (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
979	  %       ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
980	  %           (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
981	  %             MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite
982	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (single-variable elimination).}
983	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(n \in \mathbb{N}\), and \(p \in R[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) a
984	  polynomial whose image under the isomorphism
985	  \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n] \cong (R[X_1, \dots, X_n])[X_0]\) has a \emph{unit} leading
986	  coefficient — i.e.\ \(p\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to a unit. Then the
987	  quotient \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p)\) is module-finite over the coefficient subring
988	  \(R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) (embedded by \(X_j \mapsto X_j\), \(j = 1, \dots, n\)).
989	\end{lemma}

991	\begin{proof}
992	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
993	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (single-variable elimination).}
994	  Write \(S := R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) and let
995	  \(\Phi : R[X_0, \dots, X_n] \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } S[X_0]\) be the standard \(R\)-algebra
996	  isomorphism, which sends \(X_0\) to the univariate variable and embeds \(S\) as the constants. Put \(q := \Phi(p) \in S[X_0]\); by hypothesis its leading
997	  coefficient \(u\) is a unit, so the associate \(\tilde q := u^{-1} \cdot q\) is monic and
998	  \((\tilde q) = (q)\) in \(S[X_0]\). The isomorphism \(\Phi\) carries the ideal \((p)\) onto
999	  \((q) = (\tilde q)\), hence descends to a ring isomorphism
1000	  \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p) \cong S[X_0]/(\tilde q)\) that is \(S\)-linear, because \(S\) sits
1001	  inside both sides as the constants (respectively as the coefficient subring
1002	  \(R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\)). By \cref{lem:polynomial_monic_quotient_finite} the quotient
1003	  \(S[X_0]/(\tilde q)\) is module-finite over \(S\); transporting finiteness along the
1004	  \(S\)-linear isomorphism shows \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p)\) is module-finite over
1005	  \(S = R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
1006	\end{proof}

1044	  %                 (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
1045	  %         Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1046	  %           (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)
1047	  %   one may always take m' = d - 1 (single-variable elimination); the strong induction
1048	  %   accepts any m' < d.
1049	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (support-dimension drop).}
1050	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(d \ge 1\), and \(T\) a finite \emph{torsion} module
1051	  over \(P_d := A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) (with its induced \(A\)-module structure). Then there
1052	  exist a non-zero \(g \in A\) and an integer \(m' < d\) such that, after inverting \(g\),
1053	  the localised module \(T_g := T[g^{-1}]\) is module-finite over the polynomial ring
1054	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) in \(m'\) variables over the base ring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\);
1055	  that is, \(T_g\) carries compatible \(A_g\)- and \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)-module
1056	  structures (via the scalar tower \(A_g \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)) and is finite over
1057	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\). One may always take \(m' = d - 1\).
1058	\end{lemma}
1059	
1060	\begin{proof}
1061	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
1062	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop).}
1063	  \uses{lem:gf_torsion_annihilator, lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar,
1064	        lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic, lem:gf_pullback_module_transport,
1065	        lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv, lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars}
1066	  Write \(d = (d-1) + 1\) and \(P_d = A[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\) (the distinguished variable
1067	  \(X_0\) is the one eliminated in the single-variable step; cf.\ \cref{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}).
1068	
1069	  \emph{Annihilator.} By \cref{lem:gf_torsion_annihilator} there is a non-zero
1070	  \(F \in \operatorname{Ann}_{P_d}(T)\), and \(T\) is a finite module over \(P_d/(F)\).
1071	
1072	  \emph{Nagata normalisation.} Applying \cref{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar} to \(F \ne 0\)
1073	  yields a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(P_d\) and a non-zero
1074	  \(g \in A\) such that the image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\) has a unit
1075	  leading coefficient in \(X_0\). The automorphism \(e\) induces a ring isomorphism
1076	  \(P_d/(F) \cong P_d/(e\,F)\), so \(T\) is also finite over \(P_d/(e\,F)\). Localising at
1077	  the powers of \(g\) (localisation commutes with the quotient), \(T_g\) is finite over
1078	  \(P_d[g^{-1}]/(e\,F) = A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\).
1079	
1080	  \emph{Elimination.} Apply the shared engine
1081	  \cref{lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic} with \(R = A_g\) and \(p\) the image of
1082	  \(e(F)\) (whose leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is a unit): the quotient
1083	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\) is module-finite over the coefficient subring
1084	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{d-1}]\), a polynomial ring in \(m' := d - 1\) variables over
1085	  \(A_g\).
1086	
1087	  \emph{Localisation transport.} The elimination step records module-finiteness of the
1088	  \emph{quotient ring} over its coefficient subring, but the conclusion of the lemma is
1089	  about a localisation of the torsion module \(T\). Making the two agree is the
1090	  mathematical content of this step, which proceeds as follows.
1091	
1092	  Two distinct localisations of \(T\) are in play and must be identified. The natural
1093	  setting for the elimination is the localisation \(T'_g := T[\,\mathcal{C}^{-1}]\) of \(T\)
1094	  at the multiplicative set \(\mathcal{C} = \{\,c \cdot g^{\,k} : k \ge 0\,\}\) of constant
1095	  polynomials \(g^k\) inside \(P_d\); this localisation carries, for free, the full
1096	  module structure over the localised polynomial ring
1097	  \(P_d[g^{-1}] = A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\), its finiteness, and the quotient structure by
1098	  \((e\,F)\). The \emph{goal}, however, is phrased with \(T_g := T[g^{-1}]\), the
1099	  localisation of \(T\) at the powers of the single element \(g \in A\). One first proves
1100	  \(T'_g\) module-finite over \(R := A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) and then transports that
1101	  finiteness to \(T_g\).
1102	
1103	  The change of variables \(e\) is transported to the localised polynomial ring as a
1104	  \emph{ring isomorphism} \(\bar e : P_d[g^{-1}] \xrightarrow{\sim} P_d[g^{-1}]\), obtained
1105	  by localising \(e\) at \(\mathcal{C}\) (which \(e\) preserves, since \(e\) fixes the
1106	  constants \(c\,X^0\)); it carries the localised annihilator \(F_g\) to the
1107	  Nagata-normalised generator \(G\) and fixes the constants coming from the base. Passing
1108	  to quotients, \(\bar e\) descends to a ring isomorphism
1109	  \(\psi : P_d[g^{-1}]/(F_g) \xrightarrow{\sim} P_d[g^{-1}]/(G)\) of the two quotient rings
1110	  (using that the image of the ideal \((F_g)\) under \(\bar e\) is \((G)\)). Both quotient
1111	  rings act on \(T'_g\), and module-finiteness over \(R\) of the elimination-side quotient
1112	  \(P_d[g^{-1}]/(G)\) is transported across \(\psi\) — a ring isomorphism of the
1113	  \emph{acting} ring compatible with the fixed \(R\)-algebra structure — by
1114	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv}, yielding \(T'_g\) module-finite over \(R\).
1115	
1116	  Finally the two localisations are identified. Restricting scalars of the canonical
1117	  \(P_d\)-localisation map of \(T\) along \(A \to P_d\) exhibits \(T'_g\) as a localisation
1118	  of \(T\) \emph{at the powers of \(g\) over \(A\)} — this descent of the
1119	  localised-module property along the scalar tower is
1120	  \cref{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars} — so the universal property produces a
1121	  linear isomorphism \(T_g \cong T'_g\) identifying the two.
1122	
1123	  \emph{Action-diamond subtlety.} The conclusion of the lemma requires an
1124	  \(A_g\)-module structure on \(T_g\) sitting in a scalar tower
1125	  \(A_g \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}] \to T_g\). One cannot simply install an anonymous
1126	  \(A_g\)-action by fiat: \(T_g = T[g^{-1}]\) already carries its \emph{canonical}
1127	  localisation action of \(A_g\), and this canonical action is the one the scalar-tower
1128	  requirement sees. Hence the identification \(T_g \cong T'_g\) must be promoted to an
1129	  \(A_g\)-linear isomorphism — which it is, because the transported action agrees with the
1130	  canonical localisation action on the \(A_g\)-constants: the constant
1131	  \(\mathcal{C}\)-action of \(A_g\) on \(T'_g\) factors through the original \(A\)-action on
1132	  \(T\) exactly as the canonical \(g\)-localisation action does. With the isomorphism made
1133	  \(A_g\)-linear, the \(R\)-module structure and finiteness pull back from \(T'_g\) to
1134	  \(T_g\) by \cref{lem:gf_pullback_module_transport}, and the scalar tower
1135	  \(A_g \to R \to T_g\) follows.
1136	
1137	  \emph{Transitivity.} Composing the two module-finite extensions, \(T_g\) is finite over
1138	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\). Since \(m' = d - 1 < d\) (using \(d \ge 1\)), this is the
1139	  required reindexing; no Krull-dimension theory is needed, the number of variables
1140	  dropping by exactly one through the single-variable elimination.
1141	\end{proof}

1143	\subsubsection{Module-structure transport helpers (reindex)}
1144	\label{sec:gf_reindex_transport_helpers}
1145	
1146	The localisation-transport step of \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} moves a module structure
1147	and its finiteness across (i) an additive equivalence of the underlying groups, (ii) a ring
1148	isomorphism of the acting ring, and (iii) a change of localising monoid along a scalar
1149	tower. The following helper lemmas package those three transport principles. They are general
1150	module-theory facts; no external source is cited.
1151	
1152	\begin{lemma}[Module transport along an additive equivalence]
1153	  \label{lem:gf_pullback_module_transport}
1154	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
1155	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
1156	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullback_isScalarTower}
1157	  Let \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} N\) be an additive equivalence and let \(M\) carry an
1158	  \(R\)-module structure. Pulling the action back along \(e\) by
1159	  \(r \cdot y := e\big(r \cdot e^{-1}(y)\big)\) makes \(N\) an \(R\)-module for which \(e\)
1160	  is \(R\)-linear. Under this transported structure: (a) if \(M\) is module-finite over
1161	  \(R\) then so is \(N\); and (b) if \(M\) additionally carries a compatible action of a
1162	  base ring \(A_g\) with scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to M\), then the pulled-back actions on
1163	  \(N\) again form a scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to N\).
1164	\end{lemma}
1165	\begin{proof}
1166	  The module axioms for \(y \mapsto e(r \cdot e^{-1}(y))\) follow termwise from those on
1167	  \(M\) together with the fact that \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\) are additive and mutually inverse;
1168	  by construction \(e\) is then an \(R\)-linear isomorphism. Statement (a) follows by
1169	  transporting module-finiteness across the \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\).
1170	  Statement (b) checks the scalar-tower identity
1171	  \((a\,r)\cdot y = a\cdot(r\cdot y)\) on \(N\) by transporting it across \(e\) and applying
1172	  associativity of the scalar action on \(M\).
1173	\end{proof}
1174	
1175	\begin{lemma}[Finite transport along a ring isomorphism of the acting ring]
1176	  \label{lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv}
1177	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_quotientRingEquiv}
1178	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(\psi : B_1 \xrightarrow{\sim} B_2\) be a ring
1179	  isomorphism between two \(R\)-algebras that is compatible with the structure maps, i.e.\
1180	  \(\psi \circ (\text{algebra map } R \to B_1) = (\text{algebra map } R \to B_2)\). If
1181	  \(B_2\) is module-finite over \(R\) and \(M\) is module-finite over \(B_1\) (with the
1182	  scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\)), then \(M\) is module-finite over \(R\).
1183	\end{lemma}
1184	\begin{proof}
1185	  Compatibility of \(\psi^{-1}\) with the structure maps follows from that of \(\psi\), so
1186	  \(\psi^{-1}\) is an \(R\)-algebra isomorphism; transporting \(R\)-finiteness of \(B_2\)
1187	  across it makes \(B_1\) module-finite over \(R\). Transitivity of module-finiteness through
1188	  the scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\) then gives \(M\) module-finite over \(R\).
1189	\end{proof}
1190	
1191	\begin{lemma}[Descent of a localised-module structure along a scalar tower]
1192	  \label{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars}
1193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars}
1194	  Let \(A_g \to R\) be a ring map with compatible \(A_g\)- and \(R\)-module structures on
1195	  \(M\) and \(M'\) (scalar towers \(A_g \to R \to M\) and \(A_g \to R \to M'\)), and let
1196	  \(S \subseteq A_g\) be a multiplicative subset. If an \(R\)-linear map
1197	  \(f : M \to M'\) realises \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the image submonoid
1198	  \(S \cdot 1 \subseteq R\) over \(R\), then its restriction of scalars to \(A_g\) realises
1199	  \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at \(S\) over \(A_g\).
1200	\end{lemma}
1201	\begin{proof}
1202	  One verifies the universal property of a localised module for the restricted map. Each
1203	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(M'\): its action coincides, via the scalar tower, with
1204	  that of its image in \(R\), which is a unit because \(f\) is a localisation at the image
1205	  submonoid. Surjectivity onto \(M'\) by fractions \(x/s\) with \(s \in S\) and the
1206	  equality criterion likewise descend from the corresponding image-submonoid statements,
1207	  rewriting each \(R\)-scalar by an \(A_g\)-scalar through the scalar tower. Assembling
1208	  these conditions gives the claim.
1209	\end{proof}
1210	
1211	\begin{lemma}[Localisation identification across an associated denominator]
1212	  \label{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated}
1213	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated}
1214	  \mathlibok
1215	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1216	  Let \(R \to S \to T\) be a tower of commutative rings with compatible structure maps, let
1217	  \(x, z \in R\) and \(y \in S\), and suppose \(S\) is the localisation of \(R\) away from
1218	  \(x\) and \(T\) is the localisation of \(S\) away from \(y\). If the image of \(z\) in \(S\)
1219	  is associated to \(y\) (the two differ by a unit), then \(T\) is the localisation of \(R\)
1220	  away from the single product \(x z\); that is, \(T \cong R[(x z)^{-1}]\).
1221	\end{lemma}

1223	\begin{lemma}[Freeness transports across a semilinear ring isomorphism]
1224	  \label{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1225	  \lean{Module.Free.of_ringEquiv}
1226	  \mathlibok
1227	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1228	  Let \(R, R'\) be rings, \(M\) an \(R\)-module and \(M'\) an \(R'\)-module, and let
1229	  \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) be an additive isomorphism that is semilinear over a ring
1230	  isomorphism \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\), i.e.\ \(e(r \cdot m) = \sigma(r) \cdot e(m)\).
1231	  If \(M\) is free over \(R\), then \(M'\) is free over \(R'\).
1232	\end{lemma}

1234	\begin{lemma}[L5 step-4 helper — descent of freeness across a tower of \(\mathrm{Away}\) localisations]
1235	  \label{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1236	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower}
1237	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1238	  % LEAN DEPS: the localisation-transport primitives the Lean proof consumes are
1239	  %   IsLocalization.surj                          (clear the denominator of h over A_g)
1240	  %   IsLocalization.map_units                     (the cleared denominator s ∈ powers g is a unit)
1241	  %   IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated        (key ring identification: Away(g·a) on A_h)
1242	  %   IsLocalization.algEquiv                      (ring iso Away(g·a) ≅ A_h)
1243	  %   Module.Free.of_ringEquiv                     (transport freeness across that ring iso)
1244	  %   IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv                (the σ-semilinear module equivalence)
1245	  %   LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization    (upgrade the R-linear equiv to Away(g·a)-linear)
1246	  % The one primitive NOT packaged by Mathlib — that the composite localisation map
1247	  %   ψ := mkLinearMap(powers h) ∘ mkLinearMap(powers g) : T → (T_g)_h
1248	  % satisfies IsLocalizedModule (powers (g·a)) ψ — is proved directly from the three
1249	  % IsLocalizedModule axioms (units act invertibly, surjectivity up to denominators, the
1250	  % equality criterion); there is no packaged localisation-of-localisation lemma to invoke.
1251	  % NOTE: the closed Lean proof DID find a packaged route — the composite-localisation
1252	  % `IsLocalizedModule (powers (g·a)) ψ` step goes through `IsBaseChange.comp` via
1253	  % `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange`, so the "no packaged lemma" remark above is superseded.
1254	  % WITNESS: the conclusion holds at the SINGLE product f := g·a (not at a square f²).
1255	  Let \(A\) be a domain and \(T\) an \(A\)-module. Let \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), and let
1256	  \(h \in A_g := \mathrm{Away}(g)\) be non-zero. Write
1257	  \((T_g)_h\) for the iterated localisation of \(T\) — first away from \(g\), then away from
1258	  \(h\) — and \((A_g)_h := \mathrm{Away}(h)\) for the corresponding iterated localisation of the
1259	  base ring. If \((T_g)_h\) is free over \((A_g)_h\), then there exists \(f \in A\),
1260	  \(f \ne 0\), such that the single localisation \(T_f\) is free over \(A_f := \mathrm{Away}(f)\).
1261	  Concretely the witness is the single product \(f := g\,a\) for an element \(a \in A\) whose
1262	  image in \(A_g\) is associated to \(h\).
1263	\end{lemma}
1264	
1265	\begin{proof}
1266	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1267	  \emph{Clear the denominator of \(h\).} As \(h\) lies in \(A_g = \mathrm{Away}(g)\),
1268	  surjectivity of the localisation map \(A \to A_g\) onto fractions yields \(a \in A\) and a
1269	  power \(s\) of \(g\) with \(h \cdot \bar s = \bar a\) in \(A_g\), where \(\bar{\,\cdot\,}\)
1270	  denotes the image in \(A_g\). The image \(\bar s\) is a unit (a power of \(g\) is invertible
1271	  in \(A_g\)), so \(\bar a = h\,\bar s\) is associated to \(h\); in particular \(\bar a \ne 0\)
1272	  (because \(h \ne 0\)), hence \(a \ne 0\). Since \(A\) is a domain and \(g, a \ne 0\), the
1273	  product \(f := g\,a\) is non-zero.
1274	
1275	  \emph{Identify the base rings.} The associatedness of \(\bar a\) and \(h\), together with the
1276	  tower \(A \to A_g \to A_h\), identifies the iterated localisation
1277	  \((A_g)_h = \mathrm{Away}(h)\) with the single localisation of \(A\) away from the product
1278	  \(g\,a\): by \cref{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated} (with \(x = g\), \(z = a\),
1279	  \(y = h\)) the ring \(A_h\) is a localisation of \(A\) away from \(g\,a\), and uniqueness of
1280	  localisation furnishes a ring isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \xrightarrow{\sim} A_h\).
1281	
1282	  \emph{Identify the modules.} The composite localisation map
1283	  \[
1284	    \psi : T \longrightarrow (T_g)_h, \qquad
1285	    \psi := \mathrm{mk}_{\langle h\rangle} \circ \mathrm{mk}_{\langle g\rangle},
1286	  \]
1287	  first into \(T_g\) and then into \((T_g)_h\), realises \((T_g)_h\) as the localisation of
1288	  \(T\) at the powers of \(g\,a\): every \(g^n a^m\) acts invertibly on \((T_g)_h\), \(\psi\) is
1289	  surjective up to such denominators, and its kernel is the expected one. This
1290	  localisation-of-localisation identity is verified directly from the defining properties of a
1291	  localised module; no packaged lemma supplies it.
1292	
1293	  \emph{Transport freeness.} By hypothesis \((T_g)_h\) is free over \(A_h\). The ring
1294	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \cong A_h\) and the induced semilinear module equivalence
1295	  \(T_{g\,a} \simeq (T_g)_h\) — assembled from the localised-module identification of the
1296	  previous step and promoted to an \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\)-linear equivalence — carry this
1297	  freeness back by \cref{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}: \(T_{g\,a}\) is free over
1298	  \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\). Taking \(f := g\,a\) gives \(T_f\) free over \(A_f\), as required.
1299	\end{proof}

1342	  %        ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)

1344	  % by Nat.strong_induction_on d, with A (and its CommRing/IsDomain/IsNoetherianRing
1345	  % instances) AND all of N's d-dependent module instances REVERTED INTO THE MOTIVE.
1346	  % This is load-bearing, and is the real cause of the prior stall: the reindex
1347	  % lem:gf_torsion_reindex changes the base ring from A to A_g = Localization.Away g, so
1348	  % the inductive hypothesis must be applied at base A_g, NOT A. An IH that fixes A would
1349	  % not typecheck on the reindexed module T_g (which is finite over
1350	  % MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g). A_g remains a noetherian domain (a localisation of a
1351	  % noetherian domain), so the motive's hypotheses hold at A_g. Because `A` is already an
1352	  % explicit argument of the pinned signature, this is a reversion of A into the motive,
1353	  % NOT a change to the public signature. See analogies/gf-generic-rank-ses.md.
1354	  We argue by strong induction on \(d\), \emph{generalising the base domain \(A\)} (along
1355	  with the module \(N\)): the inductive hypothesis is available for every \(d' < d\),
1356	  every noetherian domain in place of \(A\), and every finite module over the
1357	  corresponding polynomial ring.
1358	
1359	  \emph{Base case \(d = 0\).} The ring \(P_0 = A[X_1, \dots, X_0]\) is \(A\) itself, so
1360	  \(N\) is a finite \(A\)-module and the claim is exactly the finite-module leaf
1361	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}.
1362	
1363	  \emph{Inductive step \(d \ge 1\).} If \(N\) is already a torsion \(P_d\)-module over
1364	  \(A\) — equivalently \(N_K = 0\) for \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) — then the torsion base
1365	  case \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} produces \(f \ne 0\) with \(N_f = 0\), which is free
1366	  over \(A_f\). Otherwise we proceed in five steps.
1367	  \begin{enumerate}
1368	    \item \textbf{Generic-rank SES.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_generic_rank_ses} to \(N\) over
1369	      \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\): there are \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) and an injective
1370	      \(P_d\)-linear map \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) with torsion cokernel
1371	      \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\), i.e.\ a short exact sequence of
1372	      \(P_d\)-modules
1373	      \[
1374	        0 \to P_d^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } N \to T \to 0,
1375	        \qquad T \text{ torsion}.
1376	      \]
1377	      No element of \(A\) is inverted at this step.
1378	    \item \textbf{Reindex the torsion cokernel.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} to the
1379	      finite torsion module \(T\): there are \(g \ne 0\) in \(A\) and \(m' < d\) such that
1380	      the localisation \(T_g\) is module-finite over the polynomial ring
1381	      \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) in fewer variables over the base ring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\).
1382	    \item \textbf{Inductive hypothesis at the new base \(A_g\).} Since \(A_g\) is again a
1383	      noetherian domain and \(m' < d\), the inductive hypothesis applies \emph{at base
1384	      \(A_g\)} to the finite \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)-module \(T_g\): there is
1385	      \(h \ne 0\) in \(A_g\) with \((T_g)_h\) free over \((A_g)_h\). This is exactly the
1386	      point at which the base ring of the induction must be allowed to change from \(A\)
1387	      to \(A_g\).
1388	    \item \textbf{Descend the witness from \(A_g\) to \(A\).} Clearing the denominator of
1389	      \(h\) over \(A_g\) produces a numerator \(a \in A\) whose image in \(A_g\) is associated
1390	      to \(h\); then the iterated localisation \((A_g)_h\) is the single localisation
1391	      \(A_{g a}\) and \((T_g)_h\) is \(T_{g a}\). Thus freeness of \((T_g)_h\) over
1392	      \((A_g)_h\) transports to freeness of \(T_{g a}\) over \(A_{g a}\) by the
1393	      iterated-\(\mathrm{Away}\)-tower descent \cref{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}. Set
1394	      \(f := g a \ne 0\) — the single product, \emph{not} a square — so that \(T_f\) is free
1395	      over \(A_f\).
1396	      % NOTE: the tower-descent helper lem:gf_away_tower_descent
1397	      % (free_localizationAway_of_away_tower) is now CLOSED and axiom-clean. The L5 sorry that
1398	      % remains is NOT a missing math step — it is a Lean elaboration blocker: the
1399	      % `OreLocalization` instance *presentations* on the localised quotient `(N ⧸ range φ)_g`
1400	      % (CommSemiring / AddCommMonoid / Module-SMul) emitted by `gf_torsion_reindex` are defeq
1401	      % but not instance-transparent-equal to the presentations the helper input expects. The
1402	      % clean fix is non-local: make `gf_torsion_reindex` emit its conclusion over the canonical
1403	      % `OreLocalization.*` instances (its final `exact` / existential binders), after which the
1404	      % recorded five-line assembly closes verbatim. Do NOT re-dispatch a raw L5 prover round
1405	      % without that instance-alignment refactor first.
1406	    \item \textbf{Splice.} The left end \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) of the sequence is a free
1407	      \(A\)-module (the polynomial ring \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) is \(A\)-free, hence
1408	      so is any finite direct power; note \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) is \emph{not} module-finite
1409	      over \(A\), but freeness is all the splice requires), and a free module stays free
1410	      under the localisation \(A \to A_f\); so \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) localises to a free
1411	      \(A_f\)-module. With both ends free over \(A_f\) after inverting \(f = g a\),
1412	      \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact} splices the localised short exact sequence to
1413	      conclude that the middle term \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Taking \(f = g a\)
1414	      completes the induction.
1415	  \end{enumerate}
1416	\end{proof}

1427	[Flat localization away, finite module]

1429	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite}
1430	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}
1431	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(M\) a finite \(A\)-module. Then there exists
1432	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(M_f\) is flat over \(A_f\).
1433	\end{lemma}
1434	\begin{proof}
1435	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}

1445	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}

1482	  %   theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
1483	  %       (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
1484	  %       [F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType] :
1485	  %       ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧ (the fibrewise flatness conclusion,
1486	  %       quantified over affine opens U ≤ V of S and affine opens W ≤ p⁻¹U of X, with
1487	  %       Γ(F,W) given its Γ(S,U)-module structure via p.appLE — verbatim form in the
1488	  %       .lean file, the source of truth).
1489	
1490	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
1491	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
1492	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1493	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
1494	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
1495	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
1496	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
1497	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
1498	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
1499	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1500	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
1501	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
1502	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
1503	
1504	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
1505	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).
1506	\end{theorem}
1507	
1508	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
1509	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
1510	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
1511	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
1512	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
1513	% by restricting to a non-empty
1514	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
1515	\begin{proof}
1516	  
1517	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
1518	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
1519	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
1520	
1521	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is locally of finite
1522	  type it is in particular quasi-compact, so the open subscheme \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\)
1523	  is quasi-compact; choose a finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of
1524	  \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each
1525	  ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\) is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
1526	
1527	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
1528	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
1529	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-type hypothesis makes \(M_j\)
1530	  a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\), \(M_j\) is
1531	  the data fed to the algebraic form.
1532	
1533	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
1534	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
1535	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
1536	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
1537	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
1538	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1539	
1540	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
1541	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the fibrewise flatness conclusion then follows from
1542	  these local freeness statements by the criterion that a module flat at every maximal
1543	  ideal is flat. This produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
1544	\end{proof}

1839	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
1840	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
1841	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
1842	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1843	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
1844	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
1845	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
1846	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
1847	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
1848	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
1849	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
1850	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
1851	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
1852	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
1853	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
1854	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
1855	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
1856	  | _ d IH =>
1857	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
1858	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
1859	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
1860	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
1861	      subst hd
1862	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
1863	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
1864	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
1865	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
1866	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
1867	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
1868	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
1869	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
1870	        -- ring of definition.
1871	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
1872	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
1873	        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
1874	        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
1875	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1876	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1877	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1878	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1879	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1880	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1881	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1882	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1883	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1884	        --
1885	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1886	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1887	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1888	        --
1889	        -- The generic-rank SES `0 → (Fin m → P) → N → T → 0` (`gf_generic_rank_ses`),
1890	        -- with `T := N ⧸ range φ` torsion over `P := A[X_1,…,X_d]`.
1891	        obtain ⟨m, φ, hφinj, hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
1892	        -- Step 1: the torsion cokernel `N ⧸ range φ` is a finite `P`-module (its restricted
1893	        -- `A`-module structure + scalar tower `A → P → quotient` are found by instances).
1894	        haveI hTfin : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) :=
1895	          Module.Finite.of_surjective (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1896	        -- Step 2: reindex the torsion cokernel onto `m' < d` variables over `A_g`.
1897	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` now emits its `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-action (`hmod1`) and the
1898	        -- scalar tower (`htower`) over the *canonical* (`inferInstance`) `Module A_g`-action on the
1899	        -- localised module — the redundant `Module A_g T_g` existential was dropped, so the
1900	        -- `A_g`-action the IH and `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` synthesise is exactly the
1901	        -- one `htower` refers to.  This dissolves the former `OreLocalization`
1902	        -- instance-presentation diamond: there is now a single `Module A_g T_g` instance in play.
1903	        obtain ⟨g, hg0, m', hm'lt, hmod1, htower, hfin⟩ :=
1904	          gf_torsion_reindex A d hd (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hTtors
1905	        -- Step 3: the inductive hypothesis at the new noetherian-domain base `A_g`.
1906	        haveI hdomg : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
1907	          IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
1908	            (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0))
1909	        haveI hnoethg : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) := inferInstance
1910	        -- Register the reindex's `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-module structure, its finiteness, and
1911	        -- its scalar tower in the local instance cache so the IH application infers them.
1912	        letI := hmod1
1913	        haveI := hfin
1914	        haveI := htower
1915	        -- Steps 3–4: the IH at base `A_g` produces `hh ≠ 0` in `A_g` with the doubly-localised
1916	        -- `(T_g)_hh` free over `(A_g)_hh`; the tower-descent helper
1917	        -- `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` descends that witness to a single `f := g·a ∈ A`.
1918	        obtain ⟨f, hf0, hTf_free⟩ :
1919	            ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1920	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)) := by
1921	          obtain ⟨hh, hh0, hfree_Tgh⟩ :=
1922	            IH m' hm'lt (Localization.Away g)
1923	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ))
1924	          exact free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hg0 hh0 hfree_Tgh
1925	        -- Step 5: splice the localised SES.  The free left end `Fin m → P` (a finite power
1926	        -- of the `A`-free polynomial ring `P`) localises to a free `A_f`-module by base
1927	        -- change, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices.
1928	        haveI hM'free_base : Module.Free A (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) := inferInstance
1929	        have hM'_free : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1930	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1931	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1932	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1933	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1934	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1935	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1936	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1937	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free
1938	
1939	end GenericFreeness

1941	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1942	
1943	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1944	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1945	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1946	
1947	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
1948	
1949	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1950	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1951	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1952	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1953	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1954	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1955	
1956	* **Surviving residue** (`sorry` this iter): when `M` is finite over the
1957	  *finite-type* algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A`, the genuine §4
1958	  dévissage is required — a prime filtration of `M` as a finite `B`-module
1959	  reduces to `M = B/𝔭`, Noether normalisation makes `B_g` finite over the
1960	  polynomial ring `A_g[b₁,…,b_n]`, and induction on the support dimension
1961	  bottoms out at the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness. That core
1962	  (a finite module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]` is generically free) is the precise
1963	  piece Mathlib does not yet supply. -/
1964	
1965	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1966	-- The §4 dévissage assembly in the `B/𝔭` branch (Noether normalisation, the
1967	-- polynomial-ring core, and the ring↔module localisation bridge) drives the deep
1968	-- localisation/quotient instance stack past the default elaboration and
1969	-- instance-search heartbeat limits; both are raised for this declaration.
1970	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
1971	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1972	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1973	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1974	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1975	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1976	
1977	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1978	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1979	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (Noether normalisation +
1980	the polynomial-ring core, both proved above). -/
1981	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
1982	    (A B M : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1983	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
1984	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
1985	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
1986	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1987	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1988	  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
1989	  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
1990	    haveI := hAM
1991	    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
1992	  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not
1993	    -- module-finite over `A`. Dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
1994	    -- (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) with motive
1995	    -- `P N := ∃ f ≠ 0, Free A_f (N_f)`, each `B`-module `N` carrying its restricted
1996	    -- `A`-action through `algebraMap A B`. The subsingleton obligation discharges
1997	    -- from the torsion base case (L1); the short-exact obligation from the L3
1998	    -- splice; the `N ≅ B/𝔭` obligation is the genuine residual (it needs L4
1999	    -- finiteness + L5, the polynomial-ring core still under construction).
2000	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B
2001	    have key : letI : Module A M := Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B)
2002	        ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2003	          Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
2004	      refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime B
2005	        (inferInstance : Module.Finite B M)
2006	        (motive := fun N _ _ _ => letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2007	          ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2008	            Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N))
2009	        ?_ ?_ ?_
2010	      · -- subsingleton `N` ⟹ torsion base case (L1).
2011	        intro N _ _ _ _
2012	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2013	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2014	        refine GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N ?_
2015	        exact LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m =>
2016	          ⟨1, Submonoid.one_mem _, by rw [Subsingleton.elim m 0, smul_zero]⟩
2017	      · -- `N ≅ B/𝔭` (domain quotient, finite-type over `A`): the genuine residual.
2018	        -- L4 (`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`) and L5
2019	        -- (`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`) are now BOTH closed and
2020	        -- axiom-clean, so the remaining work here is pure assembly:
2021	        --   1. Set `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal` (a finite-type `A`-algebra DOMAIN, via
2022	        --      `Ideal.Quotient.isDomain` + `Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective`), and
2023	        --      transport the goal from `N` to `C` along the `B`-linear (hence
2024	        --      `A`-linear) equiv `a`, since freeness through `LocalizedModule` is
2025	        --      preserved by an `A`-linear equivalence.
2026	        --   2. Split on `Subsingleton (C ⊗_A K)` (`K = Frac A`).  If torsion, close
2027	        --      with the L1 leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N`.
2028	        --   3. Otherwise apply L4 to get `g ≠ 0`, `n`, and an injective `A_g`-algebra
2029	        --      map `φ : A_g[X_n] → C_g` with `C_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2030	        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_n]` (via `φ.toAlgebra`).  Feed `C_g` (as a
2031	        --      finite `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`-module) to L5 at base `A_g`, obtaining
2032	        --      `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(C_g)_h` free over `(A_g)_h`.
2033	        --   4. Bridge the ring-localisation `C_g = Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2034	        --      to the module-localisation `LocalizedModule (powers g) C` (an
2035	        --      `IsLocalizedModule`-uniqueness iso), then descend the `A_g`-witness `h`
2036	        --      to a single `f ∈ A` via `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower`.
2037	        -- The only genuinely fiddly step is the ring↔module localisation bridge in (4);
2038	        -- everything else is direct application of the now-closed L1/L4/L5 leaves.
2039	        intro N _ _ _ p a
2040	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2041	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2042	        classical
2043	        -- Step 1. Pass to the domain quotient `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal`, a finite-type
2044	        -- `A`-algebra domain. `a` transports the goal between `N` and `C`.
2045	        haveI hp : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
2046	        haveI : IsDomain (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p.asIdeal
2047	        haveI : Algebra.FiniteType A (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) :=
2048	          Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective
2049	            (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ A p.asIdeal) (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_surjective A p.asIdeal)
2050	        suffices hC : ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
2051	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal)) by
2052	          obtain ⟨f, hf, hCfree⟩ := hC
2053	          refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
2054	          haveI := hCfree
2055	          -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) C ≃ₗ[A_f] LocalizedModule (powers f) N`,
2056	          -- the localisation functor applied to `a.symm`, transfers freeness.
2057	          exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2058	            (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers f)
2059	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2060	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N)
2061	              (Localization.Away f) ((a.restrictScalars A).symm))
2062	        -- Step 2. Torsion / non-torsion split on `C_K = K ⊗_A C`.
2063	        by_cases htors :
2064	            Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2065	        · -- Torsion: the L1 leaf with `B := M := C`.
2066	          exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A
2067	            (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) htors
2068	        · -- Step 3–4. Non-torsion: Noether normalisation (L4) + polynomial core
2069	          -- (L5) + the ring↔module bridge + descent (`away_tower`).
2070	          set C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal with hCdef
2071	          -- `C_K = K ⊗_A C` is nontrivial (the non-torsion hypothesis), via the
2072	          -- identification `LocalizedModule (A⁰) C ≃ K ⊗_A C`.
2073	          haveI : Nontrivial (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) C) :=
2074	            not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial.mp htors
2075	          have hCK : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) C) :=
2076	            (LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct (nonZeroDivisors A) C).injective.nontrivial
2077	          -- Step 3. Noether normalisation: invert `g` so that `C_g` is module-finite
2078	          -- over `A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through the injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ`.
2079	          obtain ⟨n, g, hg, algBg, φ, hφinj, hφfin, hcompat⟩ :=
2080	            GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial A C hCK
2081	          set Cg := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g) with hCgdef
2082	          letI := algBg
2083	          letI := φ.toAlgebra
2084	          haveI : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg := hφfin
2085	          haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
2086	            IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors _
2087	              (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg))
2088	          haveI : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) :=
2089	            IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing (Submonoid.powers g) _ inferInstance
2090	          haveI : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
2091	              (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg :=
2092	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => (φ.commutes r).symm
2093	          -- Polynomial core (L5) at base `A_g`: invert `h ∈ A_g` to free `C_g`.
2094	          obtain ⟨h, hh, hCgfree⟩ :=
2095	            GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (Localization.Away g) n Cg
2096	          -- Step 4. Bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) C ≃ₗ[A_g] C_g`. The ambient
2097	          -- `A → C → C_g` tower exhibits `C_g` as the localisation of the `A`-module
2098	          -- `C` at `powers g` (instances inferred); `hcompat` aligns the `A_g`-action
2099	          -- with it, upgrading the `A`-linear bridge to an `A_g`-linear one.
2100	          haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away g) Cg :=
2101	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun x => (hcompat x).symm
2102	          let e0 : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[A] Cg :=
2103	            IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
2104	              (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom A C Cg).toLinearMap
2105	          let e : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[Localization.Away g] Cg :=
2106	            LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g)
2107	              (Localization.Away g) e0
2108	          -- (v) lift through `LocalizedModule (powers h) (·)` and transfer L5 freeness.
2109	          have hfree' : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
2110	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)
2111	                (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C)) := by
2112	            haveI := hCgfree
2113	            exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2114	              (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers h)
2115	                (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h) Cg)
2116	                (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
2117	                  (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C))
2118	                (Localization.Away h) e.symm)
2119	          -- Descend the `A_g`-witness to a single `f ∈ A`.
2120	          exact GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A C hg hh hfree'
2121	      · -- short-exact closure ⟹ the L3 splice.
2122	        intro N₁ _ _ _ N₂ _ _ _ N₃ _ _ _ i q hi hq hex hN₁ hN₃
2123	        letI : Module A N₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)
2124	        letI : Module A N₂ := Module.compHom N₂ (algebraMap A B)
2125	        letI : Module A N₃ := Module.compHom N₃ (algebraMap A B)
2126	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₁ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2127	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₂ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2128	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₃ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2129	        obtain ⟨f', hf', hF'⟩ := hN₁
2130	        obtain ⟨f'', hf'', hF''⟩ := hN₃
2131	        exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A B N₁ N₂ N₃
2132	          i q hi hq hex hf' hf'' hF' hF''
2133	    -- Transport the dévissage conclusion (restricted `A`-action via `algebraMap A B`)
2134	    -- to the ambient `A`-module structure on `M`; they agree by the scalar tower.
2135	    have hAinst : (Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B) : Module A M) = ‹Module A M› := by
2136	      refine Module.ext_iff.mpr ?_
2137	      funext a m
2138	      change algebraMap A B a • m = a • m
2139	      rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, smul_assoc, one_smul]
2140	    rw [hAinst] at key
2141	    exact key

2143	/-! ## Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
2144	
2145	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
2146	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial}` (chapter: `\lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`)

  The blueprint's `% LEAN SIGNATURE` block (added/updated in iter-022) records:
  ```
  ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
    (_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
    (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g) →ₐ[…] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
    Function.Injective φ ∧
    (letI := φ.toAlgebra; Module.Finite …) ∧
    (∀ a, algebraMap A_g B_g (algebraMap A A_g a) = algebraMap B B_g (algebraMap A B a))
  ```
  
  The Lean (lines 509–524) has:
  ```lean
  ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
    (algBg : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
    (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g) →ₐ[…] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
    Function.Injective φ ∧
    (letI := φ.toAlgebra; Module.Finite … ) ∧
    (letI := algBg; ∀ a : A,
      algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))
          (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a)
        = algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) (algebraMap A B a))
  ```
  
  The difference is that the blueprint uses an anonymous binder `(_ : Algebra …)` while the Lean uses a named binder `(algBg : Algebra …)` — required to allow `letI := algBg` in the 4th conjunct. The blueprint's `% NOTE (iter-022)` correctly explains this: "a FOURTH existential conjunct was added — the chosen `algBg : Algebra A_g B_g` is COMPATIBLE with the canonical `A → B → B_g` tower."
